_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/ MD/ DNB / PG Diploma or equivalent (Obstetrics & Gynecology)
clinical_indications: Large haematoma (=/> 5cm) presenting as a painful tender swelling, bluish black in appearance, persistent pain in small hematoma
Persistently increasing size of the haematoma
Pallor/Shock, which is out of proportion to the clinical blood loss
Haematoma is causing retention of urine or tenesmus

clinical_thresholds.note: presenting as a painful tender swelling, bluish black in appearance, persistent pain in small hematoma

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission
Delivery notes (whether haematoma is formed after delivery), if available/ reason for non-availability

claim.label: Blood transfusion notes (if blood transfusion was given)

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure and advice for admission
Was haematoma =/>5 cms and required drainage?

cpd_claim.q: Investigation reports done prior to the procedure
Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge

common_queries: Haematoma size not documented as =/>5 cms, or not requiring drainage without justification.
Detailed Clinical notes missing essential information (vitals, history, local examination, etc.) at pre-authorization.
Delivery notes missing or reason for non-availability not provided.
Detailed indoor case papers not submitted at claim stage.
Operative/procedure notes missing or incomplete at claim stage.
Blood transfusion notes not provided if transfusion was administered.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia and anesthetist availability
clinical_key_pointers: History: Did she have an accident or fall?
History: Did she deliver in last few days? (Date of delivery, Place of delivery, Was episiotomy performed during delivery?)
History: Persistent, severe pain in the perineal region
History: Did she have vulval varicose veins (especially during pregnancy)?
Examination: Local examination: tender, blackish/purple swelling at the vulva
quality_assessment_parameters: Time Interval between delivery and drainage?
Proportion of hematoma to normal delivery taken place in the hospital?
contraindications: Static / non increasing Small haematoma (< 3cm) can be managed conservatively
If existing signs of infection, drainage may be done under antibiotic cover but resuturing if required, should be done after infection resolves
